LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICA RINGEL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS MR. RINGEL REQUESTED A CONTINUANCE TO SEEK COUNSEL, WHICH THE COURT DENIED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT RELIES ON ALLEGED FACTS THAT ARE NOT PART OF THE COMPLAINT. FIVE DAYS TO ANSWER THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR# 7433

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICA RINGEL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS

Appearances:
Mica Ringel (no phone number given) defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona
Ronald Schivo of Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter (956-8100) for plaintiff and responding party

Mr. Ringel requested a continuance to seek counsel, which the Court denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied because defendant relies on alleged facts that are not part of the complaint. Five days to answer the complaint. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Peppina Thompson, CSR# 7433
